challenges.title: Headings and Paragraphs
challenges.description:
Create an HTML page with an

heading, an

subheading, and two

paragraphs. Assign your code to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a page with headings and paragraphs -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a closing tag' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

subheading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a closing tag' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 2, 'Should contain at least two

paragraphs' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 2, 'Should contain at least two closing

tags'
hints: Use <h1> for the main heading and <h2> for a subheading.

Each paragraph needs its own

...

tags.

challenges.title: Heading Hierarchy with List
challenges.description: Create an HTML page with an <h1>, <h2>, and <h3> heading hierarchy, at least one paragraph, and an unordered list (<ul>) with at least three list items (<li>). Assign your code to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a page with heading hierarchy, paragraph, and list -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain at least one paragraph' assert '

    ' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an unordered list' assert '
' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a closing tag' assert html.lower().count('
  • ') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three list items'
hints: Heading tags go from <h1> (most important) to <h6> (least important).
An unordered list uses <ul> with <li> items inside it.
